verbatim_text: In fact, many experts in the field were super excited with this result, mainly because this was at least one of the first results which tried to show that you can train small models to be effective algorithms that can match performance of much bigger models by scaling test time adaptation. In fact, I figured this out later, but Sasha mentioned this on Twitter that the internal training like pipeline, the RL training pipeline and cursor resembles something similar to what we had for QDNano because they have these very long traces for coding problems. And we run into similar issues and we handle it in the same way. This is also appreciated in the wider community working on LLMs and RL.
